content.full_text:
September 27, 1977


Beverly A. Warner
Deputy Registrar of Voters
Registration and Elections Division
1300 South Grand Avenue
Santa Ana, California 92705

Dear Ms. Warner:

This letter responds to your request for written confirmation of
advice provided during our telephone conversation of July 19, 1977.
During that conversation I answered questions posed in your letter
of June 23, 1977. In your letter you detailed the activities of
certain candidates and campaign committees, and you asked for advice
regarding the manner in which campaign disclosure statements should
be amended and what fines should be imposed. The following answers
are made in the order your questions were posed in your letter.

With regard to amending campaign disclosure statements, candidates
A and B each should file a 430 form disclosing all contributions
received and all payments made, including an itemization of contributions received from individual sources and payments made to individual sources of $50 or more. This itemization will disclose the
contributions received from committee Y and the payments made to
committee Z.

Committee Z should file one amended 420 form disclosing all contributions received and all payments made, including an itemization
of contributions received from individual sources of $50 or more.
This itemization will disclose the contributions received from
candidates A and B. Because committee Z is not a controlled committee, neither candidate A nor B should sign committee Z's 420
form.

With regard to the question of what fines should be imposed on
candidates A and B and committee Z, you indicated that your office
and the district attorney sent late notice to the filers, and
that your office finally received thirty-nine days after the filing deadline, two 420 forms from committee Z (one signed by candidate A and the other signed by candidate H). Further, you indicated
during a recent telephone conversation with me that candidates A
and B and committee Z have filed amended statements according to
the procedure suggested above, and the amended statements disclosed
no additional activity beyond that disclosed in the original statement.

because the original campaign disclosure statements were filed
thirty-nine days late, the staff of the Commission has concluded
that candidates A and B and committee Z each should be fined
$390. Government Code Section 91013(a) requires a filing officer
to impose late fines of $10 per day if campaign disclosure state-
ments have not been filed within five days after the filing officer
has sent specific written notice of the filing requirement. The
period for imposing fines does not extend beyond thirty-nine
days, although the statements were submitted incorrectly, because
the amended statements disclose no additional activity. Consequent-
ly, the date when the original statements were filed should be used
to end the late fining period. See 1 PPC Opinion 113 (enclosed).

Finally, you asked if two candidates could file a consolidated 490
form in connection with a joint committee they control. Government
Code Section 84213 provides that a candidate may file consolidated
statements with any committee or committees which he or she controls,
and two or more committees which act jointly may file consolidated
statements. However, the Political Reform Act does not allow two
or more candidates to file consolidated statements.

I hope the above advice is helpful. If I can be of further assis-
tance, please contact me.

Sincerely,


JOHN C. GREATWOOD
Program Coordinator

JCC:bw

Y OF RANGE

AL SERVICES AGENCY

SPECIAL SERVICES

REGISTRATION & ELECTIONS DIVISION
A E. OLSON, REGISTRAR OF VOTERS
1300 S. GRAND AVE
SANTA ANA, CA 92705
(714) 834-2244

June 23, 1977

John Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
1100 K St.
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Greenwood:

I would like a written opinion concerning the two following
questions:

1. Candidates A and B file for a school district election;
neither spend any money for their campaigns prior to the
closing date of the second pre-election period and have
filed forms 470 in compliance with those periods.

Committee Y contributes $500.00 to candidate A, and
$1000.00 to candidate B. A 48-hour contribution letter
is sent to the filing office for candidate B.

Candidates A & B give the $500.00 and $1000.00 they
have received to a group which form Committee Z.

A 410 form is sent to Secretary of State. Committee Z
supports both candidates, but neither candidate has any
control over this committee.

All money collected is spent on behalf of candidates A
and B.

At the close of filing post-election statements, neither
candidate A or B or Committee Z have filed a statement
and were notified by mail and t-lephone concerning pen-
alties. The District Attorney was notified, and a letter
sent from his office to the candidates.

Thirty-nine days after the last day to file the post-
election statement, the filing office received two (2)
420 forms from Committee Z. One reported receiving and
spending $500.00 for candidate A, and was signed by can-
didate A as a candidate controlling the committee; the
same type of report for candidate B was returned report-
ing the $1000.00 plus some other funds.

The period covering fines was stopped at this point,
however, both candidates and the committee were informed
they should amend their statements.

Question: What statements should be filed to amend all
statements?
What fine should be imposed on each party?

T. R. EGAN
DIRECTOR

11316

-2-

2. Candidates D and E form a joint committee which they
control. All funds are pooled and spent jointly.
They wish to file one consolidated form 490 for
reporting purposes.

Question: Can they use form 490 and if so how?


Yours truly,

Registrar of Voters
Orange County

by [illegible] U Warner
Deputy

sections.question:
What statements should be filed to amend all statements?
What fine should be imposed on each party?

2. Candidates D and E form a joint committee which they control. All funds are pooled and spent jointly. They wish to file one consolidated form 490 for reporting purposes.

Question: Can they use form 490 and if so how?

Yours truly,

Registrar of Voters
Orange County

By [signature] Beverly A. Warner Deputy
